agenda_pressure: Prefer metatheorems and separability results over repackaging known local classifications.
Exploit recursive exact interfaces rather than reviving false global size bounds.
Use counterexamples to motivate tightened one-sided or mixed-handed hypotheses.
Favor results with durable completeness, decidability, or fragment-interface consequences.
current_bottlenecks: The converse `SupportClosure Γ A → (Γ ⇒ A)` is still missing.
Fragment results remain atomic or restricted-context; there is no full one-sided conservativity theorem yet.
The positive explanation of the naive-support counterexample is incomplete: mixed-handedness is suggested but not yet sharply characterized.
Exact proof objects have not yet been turned into bounded search space or decidability machinery.
desired_summary_changes: Identify `SupportClosure` itself with derivability, not just with `support_ok`.
Prove arbitrary-succedent one-sided conservativity for the left-only and right-only fragments.
State an exact mixed-handed boundary criterion for when recursive support reduces to flat antecedent support.
Convert exact candidate trees into subformula-bounded finite search or decidability structure.
important_verified_counterexamples: Verified counterexample to: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {t : String}, (Γ ⇒ A) → occurs_atom t A → ∃ B ∈ Γ, occurs_atom t B
Verified counterexample to: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (Γ ⇒ A) → atom_count A ≤ atom_count_list Γ
Verified counterexample to: ∀ {s : String} {A : Tp}, ([# s] ⇒ A) → ∃ Δ : List Tp, Δ ⇒ # s ∧ A = (# s ⧸ foldl Tp.ldiv (# s) Δ) ∨ A = (foldr Tp.rdiv (# s) Δ)
missing_bridges: From `SupportClosure ↔ support_ok` and `CandidateTree ↔ derivability` to `SupportClosure ↔ derivability`.
From one-sided atomic or candidate-step collapse to arbitrary-succedent conservativity.
From mixed-handed counterexamples to an exact recovery criterion for flat support.
From exact candidate trees to a subformula-bounded finite recognition procedure.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize exact converses that identify recursive closure with derivability and then lift that control to full left-only and right-only conservativity theorems.
next_direction.label: complete_closure_then_fragment_conservativity
next_direction.rationale: CandidateTree already captures derivability globally, while verified failures of degree bounds, atom-count bounds, naive support containment, and simple singleton normal forms show that blunt global bounds will not move the frontier; progress has to come from exact recursive and fragment-sensitive metatheorems.

overexplored_patterns: Decision procedures for already-classified local fragments.
Blunt size or support bounds and nearby weakenings already blocked by counterexamples.
Fragment lemmas that stop at atomic goals or support transfer instead of exact conservativity.

canonical_targets: A deeper understanding of completeness for $L$
The boundary between cut elimination and decidability in extensions
soft_constraints: Closeness to established notation and theory
themes: Structural correspondences between language hierarchies and logical systems
Extraction of logical rules and complexity

theory_snapshot: The theory now has exact recursive interfaces at three levels: singleton sequents, atom-only contexts, and arbitrary succedents via `CandidateTree Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)`, while `SupportClosure` has been reduced exactly to `support_ok`. The main structural gap is no longer the existence of proof objects but the missing converse from closure or support to derivability, together with full left-only or right-only conservativity beyond atomic or special-context cases. Verified counterexamples to degree bounds, atom-count bounds, naive flat support containment, and simple singleton normal forms show that further progress must be recursive, fragment-sensitive, and explicit about mixed-handed obstruction.
